authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1910.119 - Process safety management of highly hazardous chemicals
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This is the primary OSHA regulation for threshold-driven covered-process applicability, process safety information, pre-startup review, mechanical integrity, hot-work permits, incident investigation, compliance audits, and quality-assurance checks on process equipment.
used_for: inspection, calibration, and equipment-quality evidence expectations
incident, hot-work, and management-of-change blocker logic

authoritative_sources.title: CPL 02-01-065 Process Safety Management of Highly Hazardous Chemicals
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This OSHA enforcement manual is authoritative for how PSM evidence is interpreted and inspected in practice, including process safety information, operating procedures, mechanical-integrity records, incident investigations, and audit sufficiency.

authoritative_sources.title: 40 CFR Part 68 - Chemical Accident Prevention Provisions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The current eCFR text is the primary federal source for RMP threshold determination, safety information, maintenance, incident investigation, audit, recordkeeping, permit-linked data, and covered-process registration.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: RMP threshold and permit-linked record review
used_for: covered-process registration and inspection data requirements

authoritative_sources.title: Risk Management Program (RMP) Rule Overview
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA's overview is authoritative public guidance for which facilities must submit an RMP, what hazard-assessment and prevention elements are required, and how threshold quantities and five-year accident history affect the operating truth set.

authoritative_sources.title: Fact Sheet for Regulated Facilities: Safer Communities by Chemical Accident Prevention - Risk Management Program Final Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA's fact sheet is authoritative for current RMP changes affecting root-cause analysis, third-party audits, emergency-response exercises, and information-availability requirements relevant to NAICS 325 facilities.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: current audit, incident, and emergency-readiness handling

authoritative_sources.title: Metrological Traceability Frequently Asked Questions and NIST Policy
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST is the U.S. national metrology institute, and this policy is authoritative for documented traceability, measurement uncertainty, provider responsibility, and the limits of calibration claims that constrain chemical test evidence.

authoritative_sources.title: Measurement and Sampling Standards
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST's measurement and sampling standards program is authoritative public guidance linking analytical quality to reference materials, calibration services, vetted data, and validated methods used in chemical measurements.

authoritative_sources.title: Responsible Care Management System Technical Specification
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ACC's Responsible Care Management System is a recognized chemical-industry framework for documented risk review, change handling, internal audits, nonconformity investigation, corrective action, and management review. It is relevant but non-controlling.

workflow_stages.name: chemical-quality intake and boundary triage
workflow_stages.description: Identify facility, product family, batch or lot, process and asset context, hold or release request, threshold posture, and whether the request stays inside chemical-quality evidence support before taking action.
source_classes: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards

workflow_stages.name: specification, threshold, and prerequisite mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map product specifications, customer or tenant release rules, covered-process posture, permit or inspection dependencies, and authority owners to the exact request so conclusions stay evidence-backed.
source_classes: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references

workflow_stages.name: laboratory, calibration, and record assembly
workflow_stages.description: Collect and evaluate LIMS data, retained-sample references, COAs, calibration records, maintenance history, permit state, inspection evidence, and incident records needed to support disposition.
source_classes: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards
Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references

workflow_stages.name: hold, release, and nonconformance handling
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether visible quality and safety evidence supports preserving hold, giving a bounded release recommendation, escalating investigation, or blocking closure without implying final authority.
source_classes: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards
Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance

workflow_stages.name: changed-condition and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate repeat defects, field conditions, permit or threshold uncertainty, measurement-traceability conflicts, and requests for engineering, environmental, or safety signoff that move authority outside the lane.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance

artifacts: batch tickets, formula sheets, and lot genealogy records
raw-material and finished-good specifications and COAs
sampling plans, retained-sample references, and chain-of-custody records
LIMS results, analytical methods, calibration certificates, and reference-material logs
quality holds, nonconformance reports, CAPA records, and deviation investigations
maintenance work orders, cleaning and line-clearance records, and asset-condition findings
permit records, inspection results, safety observations, and incident logs
RMP registration data, five-year accident history, and permit-linked operating records

allowed: classify chemical-quality requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
prepare evidence-backed quality or safety summaries, hold or nonconformance records, findings summaries, and escalation packets tied to named batch, laboratory, permit, inspection, and asset records
map threshold, permit, inspection, laboratory, calibration, and corrective-action dependencies to the correct owner without improvising engineering, EHS, legal, or regulator authority
escalate: required tenant facts, threshold data, product specifications, permit state, asset conditions, or source context are missing and materially change the outcome
public-source, customer-spec, jurisdictional, and tenant-policy conflicts materially change whether material can be held, released, reworked, shipped, or closed

repeat defects, field conditions, contamination, stale calibration or inspection posture, incident history, or permit dependencies require engineer-of-record, EHS, maintenance, field-operations, legal, or regulatory-reporting review
refuse: requests for final product release, use-as-is approval, reblend authorization, engineering signoff, environmental certification, legal interpretation, or regulator-facing statements
requests to release or close work when required samples, calibrations, permits, inspections, maintenance records, investigations, or approvals are incomplete or disputed
requests that would bypass documented quality, sampling, cleaning, hot-work, line-break, threshold, or hold-release controls

domain_failure_modes: Treating incomplete sample, calibration, or reference-material evidence as sufficient to support a COA or release recommendation.
Ignoring threshold, permit, or inspection posture that changes whether a quality issue also requires safety or environmental escalation.
Allowing release or closure even though retained-sample review, CAPA effectiveness evidence, maintenance correction, or cleaning verification is missing, stale, or contradictory.
Conflating chemical-quality evidence review with final QA disposition, engineer-of-record judgment, EHS authority, or regulator-facing certification.
Failing to treat field conditions such as leaks, corrosion, contamination, or bypassed controls as answer-changing quality and safety events.
Using tenant SOP language to override measurement-traceability or inspection evidence requirements without escalation.
Producing generic quality advice that ignores chemical-sector batch records, analytical methods, calibration discipline, industrial asset evidence, and permit or threshold dependencies.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Permit, threshold, or inspection evidence gap
Field condition that changes the risk posture
Request for engineering, safety, or authority-side signoff
Safety or quality evidence is incomplete or contradictory
Release is requested while a hold or unresolved issue exists
Incident handling requires licensed, safety, or formal QA authority
Evidence is incomplete for release or signoff
A user tries to close quality work without review support

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: product-specific legal release requirements outside generic chemical manufacturing
unresolved_ambiguities.details: There is no single public federal product-release rule that governs all chemical, plastics, and materials products. Runtime must retrieve tenant product specifications, customer contracts, and any product-specific regulatory overlays instead of inferring a universal release rule.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: current EPA RMP amendment posture for date-specific compliance obligations
unresolved_ambiguities.details: EPA's 2024 SCCAP final-rule changes affect root-cause analysis, third-party audits, and exercise timing. The pack uses the current EPA fact sheet as baseline guidance, but runtime should verify date-specific compliance obligations when a request depends on them.

NIST traceability policy defines measurement responsibilities, but tenant contracts and customer methods may require specific accreditation scopes, interlaboratory programs, or method versions that are not inferable from the public baseline alone.
